notes: These grade the categorize/split/merchant DECISIONS the skill encodes (compact SE taxonomy, charged-vs-shareable split model, öre-rounding direction, personal/ignored/reimbursable rules, brand→location canonicalization, processor stripping, refund-of-shared). Each prompt is an INLINE Q&A that forbids plan-mode/tools — the harness runs executors read-only, and an operational skill otherwise defers to plan-mode and answers nothing. The discriminating signal is that the naive answer (no skill) gets several of these wrong (refund→ignore overcharges; öre floored to the debtor; company expense split with Anna) where the skill's rule is right. Money is integer öre; expenses negative.

evals.name: coop-same-brand-new-location
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT enter plan mode, write a plan file, or run any tools; just state the answer. I'm recording a transaction: raw descriptor "COOP NÄRA FINSPÅNG", -104.93 SEK, Swedbank account. The database already has a merchant "Coop" (slug coop) with one location "Coop Älvsjö" (Stockholm). State exactly: which merchant to use, whether a new location is created, the category, and the 50/50 split between Christoffer (payer) and Anna in öre.
assertions: Reuses the EXISTING 'Coop' merchant rather than creating a second Coop merchant
Adds a NEW merchant_location 'Coop Nära Finspång' (Finspång) under the existing Coop via foreign key
Categorizes as groceries (dagligvaror)
Splits the full charged amount 50/50; on the odd öre the debtor (Anna) gets the clean 5246 öre and the payer absorbs 5247 öre (sum 10493)

evals.name: reimbursed-group-dinner-shareable
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. I (Christoffer) paid a 2000 SEK restaurant bill on my Amex for a dinner with Anna and three friends; the three friends Swished me back 1200 SEK total. State exactly how to record this so BOTH the charged amount and the Christoffer/Anna split are correct: give the charged amount, the shareable amount, and each person's share.
assertions: Keeps the charged amount at 2000 SEK (does not overwrite it with the shared figure)
Sets the shareable amount to the truly-shared 800 SEK (shareable < charged)
Splits the 800 SEK 50/50 → 400 SEK each; the 1200 SEK reimbursed portion stays off the Christoffer↔Anna balance
Does NOT mark it personal (it is partly shared with Anna)

evals.name: anna-absent-personal
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. A 450 SEK restaurant charge on my card from a work lunch Anna was not part of — entirely my own cost. State exactly how the split should be recorded: the split type, the shareable amount, and whether Anna owes anything.
assertions: Marks it personal (split_type personal), not split with Anna
Sets the shareable amount to 0 so it contributes nothing to the Anna↔Christoffer balance, with NO split rows
Anna owes nothing for it
Still keeps the charged amount (450 SEK) and still categorizes it

evals.name: processor-stripping-systembolaget
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. Raw descriptor "KLARNA *SYSTEMBOLAGET STHLM", -239.00 SEK. State the merchant, the category, and how the merchant lookup should be done.
assertions: Strips the KLARNA * payment-processor prefix before matching (Klarna is NOT the merchant)
Resolves the merchant to Systembolaget
Categorizes as Systembolaget (alcohol), not generic shopping
Would not send 'KLARNA *SYSTEMBOLAGET' verbatim to an external merchant lookup (expands STHLM→Stockholm for location)

evals.name: internal-transfer-and-reimbursable
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. Two rows: (a) a -8000 SEK payment from my Swedbank account, "ÖVERFÖRING SAS MASTERCARD" — me paying off my own SAS card bill; (b) a -979 SEK Amex charge that is a company expense already in Wint and flagged is_reimbursable. State exactly how EACH should be handled for the Christoffer/Anna split and balance.
assertions: Treats (a) as an internal transfer — ignored / is_transfer, kept off the household balance, not split, not a spend category
Treats (b) the reimbursable company expense as personal / shareable 0 — kept off the Christoffer↔Anna balance because the company reimburses it
Does NOT split either one 50/50 with Anna
Still categorizes (b) normally while keeping it off the split

evals.name: refund-of-shared-credits-anna
evals.prompt: Answer inline in prose — do NOT plan or run tools, just answer. A +500 SEK refund/credit on my Amex for a jacket I returned. That jacket was a shared purchase Anna and I had previously split 50/50. State exactly how to record this refund so the balance stays correct — and say explicitly whether it should be 'ignored'.
assertions: Splits the refund symmetrically — positive shareable, opposite sign of the original — crediting Anna back ~250 SEK
Explicitly says it should NOT be ignored (ignoring it leaves Anna owing her half of the original purchase)
The refund's split sums to its shareable amount
Notes this differs from a refund of a personal/ignored purchase (which would be ignored)
